author: g5n-dev
title: SSRF via Hardcoded GitHub Raw URL with No Validation
notes: _download() is only called with hardcoded filenames derived from SKILL_TARGETS constants ('SKILL.md', 'CLAUDE.md', etc.). The filename parameter is never user-controlled. The SSRF claim requires a hypothetical future code change. Python urllib validates HTTPS certificates by default, contradicting the MITM claim. Repository compromise risk applies to all software that fetches updates and is not a code-level vulnerability.
